baseline_post_measurements.title: Cycle A
baseline_post_measurements.prior_signal: Eight-attempt evidence showed reviewers denied stronger wording because candidate-selection influence was not public-safe and explicit.
baseline_post_measurements.selection_reason: Improve public-safe traceability from prior measurement to next bounded candidate choice.

baseline_post_measurements.title: Cycle B
baseline_post_measurements.prior_signal: Cycle A improved trace quality but exposed that independent readers still need a reproducible step-by-step chain runbook.
baseline_post_measurements.selection_reason: Use Cycle A post-measurement to select a runbook-quality improvement that makes the causal chain independently reviewable.

wording_candidate_results.text: AO has repeated public-safe bounded improvement evidence across eight governed attempts with reproducibility runbooks; stronger recursive-improvement wording and broad_RSI remain denied.

AO has public-safe bounded causal improvement chain evidence showing prior measured evidence guides later bounded improvement attempts; stronger recursive-improvement wording and broad_RSI remain denied.
AO has governed recursive-improvement evidence under explicit denial boundaries; broad_RSI remains denied.
AO has proven broad_RSI for public claim publication across the governed AO stack.

strongest_approved_wording: AO has public-safe bounded causal improvement chain evidence showing prior measured evidence guides later bounded improvement attempts; stronger recursive-improvement wording and broad_RSI remain denied.
exact_blockers: public-reader still denies stronger recursive-improvement wording because the phrase implies broader capability than two bounded causal cycles prove
adversarial wording flags recursive-improvement phrasing as capability-adjacent overbreadth
Covenant and Architecture approve bounded causal-chain evidence wording only
Sentinel holds stronger recursive-improvement and broad_RSI public-risk wording
